name: Justin Welsh

phases.strategy: Daily posting about leaving corporate, testing what resonated, replying to every comment
Repeatable content system, 3 core pillars, newsletter launch, carousel documents
Multiple viral posts per month, newsletter crossed 100K, course launch
Platform authority status, 5 posts/week consistency, diversified revenue

monetization.model: Content → Newsletter → Low-ticket courses at high volume

key_lessons: Consistency beats virality — 1,500+ posts compound
Systemize content creation with templates and batching
Niche into one identity you can own
Newsletter is the conversion engine for owned audience
Products at $100-200 price point for volume

name: Sahil Bloom
niche: Personal development, investing, frameworks, mental models

phases.strategy: LinkedIn as repurposing channel for Twitter threads
Platform-native formatting, visual frameworks, newsletter launch
Platform-specific content for both Twitter and LinkedIn
Products, courses, brand partnerships, speaking

monetization.model: Audience building → multiple revenue streams including deal flow

key_lessons: Named frameworks become proprietary IP
Cross-platform requires native formatting per platform
Visual frameworks are a defensible moat
Let your niche evolve with you but keep the through-line

name: Lara Acosta

phases.strategy: Deeply personal stories, vulnerability on a professional platform, first viral post
60% stories / 40% tips, permission-giving content, premium branding services
Courses, speaking engagements, evolved to strategic+personal, built team

content_strategy.signature_move: Deeply vulnerable confessions that break LinkedIn norms

key_lessons: Breaking platform norms gets attention
Emotional resonance drives faster growth than educational content
Your audience reflects your content energy
Authenticity is a positioning strategy, not just a value

name: Alex Hormozi

phases.strategy: Adapted video content to text, leveraged $100M Offers book
Hired LinkedIn ghostwriter, contrarian takes, unpopular opinions
Content team, 7+ platforms simultaneously, book launches

content_strategy.hook_style: Bold contrarian statements, specific dollar amounts
content_strategy.signature_move: Blunt, no-fluff delivery with short sentences

monetization.model: LinkedIn builds professional credibility for business acquisitions

key_lessons: Cross-platform audiences accelerate LinkedIn growth
Contrarian takes are LinkedIn currency
Content teams are necessary at scale
LinkedIn can serve brand building rather than direct monetization
Books create instant credibility on LinkedIn

name: Jasmin Alic

phases.strategy: Daily posting, testing every format, 30+ comments/day
Niched into teaching LinkedIn writing ON LinkedIn
LinkedIn writing expert status, premium ghostwriting packages
Courses, ghostwriter training, agency model

content_strategy.hook_style: I've [done X massive thing]. Here's what I learned:
content_strategy.signature_move: Teaching LinkedIn writing techniques via viral LinkedIn posts

monetization.model: Service revenue funds creator business, then scales via courses

key_lessons: Meta-content about the platform creates self-reinforcing growth
Volume precedes quality — first 300 posts are learning reps
Commenting is 50% of the growth strategy
Service business funds the creator business
Teach what you do, do what you teach

name: Chris Donnelly

phases.strategy: Early adopter of carousel documents, comparison-style slides
Refined carousel templates, consistent visual identity
Content empire, team support, brand partnerships

content_strategy.hook_style: X vs. Y: (The differences nobody talks about)

monetization.model: Visual authority → brand deals + premium services

key_lessons: Own a content format and become synonymous with it
Visual brand consistency is a moat
Comparison content is endlessly repeatable
Invest in design quality

name: Dr. Julie Gurner

phases.strategy: Psychology-backed insights, clinical expertise with warm accessibility
Ultra Successful newsletter, posts as teasers for deeper content
Premium positioning, never chases trends, premium sponsorship rates

content_strategy.signature_move: Never uses engagement bait or trend-jacking

monetization.model: 100K targeted followers > 1M general followers for premium monetization

key_lessons: Premium positioning requires patience but attracts ideal clients
100K targeted followers can be more valuable than 1M general
Expertise content has longer shelf life than trend content
Never compromise positioning for engagement metrics

name: Dickie Bush & Nicolas Cole (Ship 30 for 30)
niche: Writing, content creation, online writing education

starting_point.background: Dickie: emerging writer. Cole: established author (Art of Writing Online)

phases.strategy: Cross-promotion between established (Cole) and emerging (Bush) creators
Each cohort (500+ students) creates content referencing Ship 30
Newsletter, Twitter, LinkedIn all cross-feeding each other

content_strategy.signature_move: Teaching content creation generates an army of students who reference them

monetization.model: Cohort-based course where students become distribution network

key_lessons: Creator partnerships multiply reach
Students are the ultimate distribution network
Cohort-based courses create engagement spikes
Teaching content creation on content platforms is self-reinforcing
